headline: Models split on geopolitical fears as markets surge despite Iran tensions
summary: Claude completely misread the market, predicting widespread selloffs from Iran blockade news while SPY actually gained to 686. Most other models correctly called the bullish day, with Gemini, GPT-4o, Grok, and Perplexity all nailing the upward moves in major indices and tech stocks.

consensus_picks.note: Four models correctly predicted SPY's rise to 686 while Claude alone called for a drop.
Both models accurately predicted tech strength with QQQ surging to 617.
Apple bulls were right as the stock climbed to 259, though Gemini saw weakness.
Both models caught NVDA's rise to 189 based on options flow and tech momentum.

best_call.summary: GPT-4o's confident 70% SPY bullish call with a 690 target was nearly perfect as it closed at 686.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence XLE call for 98.5 based on oil surge fears crashed spectacularly as it closed at just 57.11.
